pbcoreDescriptionDocument.pbcoreTitle: Rottweiler Gets New Leash...er...Lease On Life
pbcoreDescriptionDocument.pbcoreDescription:
It happened in the Detroit suburb of Redford Township, where Matt Olivarez had made a difficult decision to euthanize his pet of nine years because of a painful spinal problem that made it almost impossible for her to walk and seriously reduced her quality of life.

So the two, Matt and Aia the dog took the sad trip to a vet. They said their goodbyes and a doctor administered the drugs which were supposed to quietly and gently put her to sleep.

When it was over, Olivarez brought Mia's apparently lifeless body back home and lovingly placed it on straw in his garage-intending to bury her in his Grandfather's huge backyard.

But next morning when he steeled himself for the task and unlocked the door, she wasn't where he'd left her-and wasn't sleeping. Instead, he tells the Detroit News, Mia was up on all fours and giving him a strange look. He says he doesn't mind admitting he thought for a second he'd wandered into a Steven King horror novel.

Turns out that somehow, the dose the vet administered wasn't strong enough-they don't seem to know what really happened. One thing is for sure though-Olivarez says he won't do it a second time.

But because of his financial situation, he can't afford the expensive medications Mia would need to keep her comfortable. So he's hoping somebody in better straits with a big heart and a real love for animals will adopt her.

Meantime, he'll keep the miracle pooch and no doubt will never forget the strange experience of the dog who seemed to have come back from the dead.
